keynotes.text: color="#800000">AMMONIUM PHOSPHORICUM Phosphate of Ammonia A remedy for chronic gouty patients uric acid diathesis, indicated in bronchitis and nodosities of the joints of the fingers and backs of the hands. Facial paralysis. Pain in shoulder-joint. Tightness around chest. Heaviness of limbs, unsteady, tottering gait. Coldness from least draft of air.

This salt has been used in cases of chronic gout with nodes of urate of soda on the joints. C. Voigt took three grains of Am. Phos., with this result: In about an hour, attacked by a sense of tightness in the precordia and around the chest; nausea; thirst; a hard, small, frequent pulse; and a collapsed state of the circulation. Afterwards, fulness and tension in head; heaviness in limbs; unsteady, tottering gait. Allen says it has been used with success in some cases of facial paralysis. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main

generalities.text: Main THE ENCYCLOPEDIA OF PURE MATERIA MEDICA By TIMOTHY F. ALLEN, A.M., M.D. Presented by Médi-T AMMONIUM PHOSPHORICUM. Authority. C. Voigt, M.D., Med. Examiner (Bost. Med. and Surg. Journ., vol. xxxiv, p. 308), effects of about 3 grains. ► In about an hour attacked by a sense of tightness in the præcordia and around the chest; nausea; thirst; a hard, small, frequent pulse; and a collapsed state of the circulation. These disturbances were followed by a fulness and tension in the head; heaviness in the limbs; and an unsteady tottering gait. Copyright © Médi-T ® 2000 Main
Clinical: Facial paralysis; Gout; Joints, concretions on

head.text: Sneezing with excessive running from nose and eyes, only in morning

respiratory.text: Deep rough cough with greenish expectoration
